reasoning: TPC-H SF10 workload with 64-core AVX512 hardware. Q1 already competitive (48ms vs DuckDB 87ms). Q3 is the primary bottleneck (386ms vs 78ms = 5x gap) due to multi-way joins. Q6 nearly optimal (29ms vs 19ms). Focus: maximize thread parallelism across all queries, optimize Q3 join strategy aggressively, leverage zone maps and dictionary encoding.

focus_areas: Thread parallelism with 64-core morsel-driven execution (10K-50K row morsels for L3 cache efficiency)
AVX512 SIMD for arithmetic operations (4 SUMs with expressions, 3 AVGs)
Dictionary-encoded l_returnflag and l_linestatus (uint8_t) for efficient grouping
Zone map pruning on l_shipdate (sorted, indexed) for date filter
Hash aggregation with pre-sized table (4 groups max) and partial aggregation per thread
Fused scan-filter-aggregate pipeline to minimize materialization
Q1.strategy_notes: Q1 is embarrassingly parallel: 60M rows → 4 groups. Already beating DuckDB (48ms vs 87ms), so focus on clean, aggressive parallelism. Use morsel-driven execution with one hash table per thread, then merge. Critical: handle dictionary encoding correctly (l_returnflag/l_linestatus are uint8_t, not strings). Use AVX512 for vectorized arithmetic on l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax. Zone map on l_shipdate should skip ~1% of blocks. Avoid over-optimization—this query is already fast.

focus_areas: Join ordering: customer (1.5M → 300K after c_mktsegment filter) → orders (15M → 4M after o_orderdate filter) → lineitem (60M → ~30M after l_shipdate filter)
Push filters BEFORE joins: c_mktsegment='BUILDING' (20% selectivity), o_orderdate < '1995-03-15' (26% selectivity), l_shipdate > '1995-03-15' (50% selectivity)
Hash join strategy: build hash table on filtered customer (300K rows, small side), probe with filtered orders (4M rows), then build on join result, probe with filtered lineitem
Parallel hash joins with thread-local build, then merge or partitioned hash join for large lineitem probe
Partial aggregation: local pre-aggregation per thread during lineitem probe, then global merge (reduces from ~3M rows to 300K groups)
Top-K heap for LIMIT 10: maintain min-heap of size 10 during aggregation, avoid full sort of 300K groups
Zone map exploitation: use o_orderdate_zonemap and l_shipdate_zonemap to skip blocks (both tables sorted by date)
Dictionary encoding for c_mktsegment (uint8_t) enables fast integer comparison
Q3.strategy_notes: Q3 is the critical bottleneck (5x slower than DuckDB). Attack vectors: (1) Join ordering—smallest filtered table first: customer 1.5M→300K (20% selectivity) is the best build side. (2) Filter pushdown—apply all three date/segment filters BEFORE joins using zone maps. (3) Join algorithm—use hash joins with proper build/probe sides: build on customer (300K), probe with orders (4M); then build on intermediate result, probe with lineitem (30M after filter). (4) Partial aggregation—don't materialize full join result (potentially 10M+ rows); aggregate during lineitem probe with thread-local hash tables, then merge to ~300K groups. (5) Top-K optimization—maintain heap of size 10 during aggregation; avoid sorting 300K groups. (6) Parallelism—partition hash join for lineitem probe (largest table), use all 64 cores. Target: close the 5x gap to reach ~100ms. Be aggressive with all optimizations simultaneously—this is the highest-impact query.

focus_areas: Thread parallelism with morsel-driven scan (64 cores, 10K-50K row morsels)
Predicate ordering by selectivity: l_shipdate range (low, ~16%), l_discount BETWEEN (low, ~20%), l_quantity < 24 (high, ~50%)
Zone map pruning on l_shipdate (sorted, indexed) to skip blocks outside [1994-01-01, 1995-01-01)
AVX512 SIMD for predicate evaluation (vectorized comparisons on dates, discount, quantity) and arithmetic (l_extendedprice * l_discount)
Columnar access: only load 3 columns (l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity, l_extendedprice)
Branch-free filtering or bitmask-based selection for tight predicate loop
Q6.strategy_notes: Q6 is a highly selective single-table scan (2.4% selectivity → ~1.4M qualifying rows from 60M). Already close to DuckDB (29ms vs 19ms). Focus: (1) Zone map pruning—l_shipdate is sorted and indexed, should skip ~84% of blocks immediately. (2) Predicate ordering—apply cheapest, most selective predicates first: date range (excludes 84%), then discount BETWEEN, then quantity. Use short-circuit evaluation. (3) SIMD—AVX512 can process 8 doubles or 16 int32s per instruction; vectorize all comparisons and the final multiplication. (4) Parallelism—60M rows / 64 cores = ~1M rows/core, with 100K block size = ~10 blocks/core. Morsel-driven execution with thread-local sum, then reduce. (5) Columnar access—only touch 4 columns, ignore other 12. Target: match or beat DuckDB's 19ms by maximizing zone map skipping and SIMD utilization.
